title: Server-side geolocation service — ip-api.com, injectable, graceful fail
acceptance_criteria: Exports type 'GeolocationResult = { country: string | null; isIndia: boolean; confidence: "high" | "low" | "unknown" }'

Exports 'getClientCountry(ip: string, fetchFn?: typeof fetch): Promise<GeolocationResult>'. Uses GEOLOCATION_API_URL env var (default 'https://ip-api.com/json') appended with '/{ip}?fields=status,country,countryCode'. fetchFn parameter allows injecting a mock in tests.
Returns confidence='high' when ip-api.com returns status='success' and countryCode is a 2-letter ISO code
Returns { country: null, isIndia: false, confidence: 'unknown' } on any fetch error, timeout (5s max), non-200 response, or malformed JSON — never throws, never rejects the calling request
Sets isIndia=true only when countryCode === 'IN' and confidence === 'high'

Exports 'extractClientIp(request: FastifyRequest): string'. Reads from request.ip (which Fastify sets correctly when trustProxy is configured). Falls back to '0.0.0.0' if undefined. Does NOT read X-Forwarded-For directly — trusts Fastify's trustProxy to resolve the correct hop.

A comment in the file explains: 'trustProxy must be set to the number of known proxy hops (e.g. 1 for Railway/Fly.io) in the Fastify instance config. Misconfiguring trustProxy is a geolocation spoofing risk (cosmetic only — IP is not used for pricing decisions, only display).'
output_format: src/payment/geolocation.ts plus plain-English note on why the injectable fetchFn exists and why the spoofing risk is acceptable
